label.purpose: Field labels and column keys; uppercase, tracked.

data.purpose: Every comparable figure: tonnages, fuel, times, subtotals.

shadows.name: modal-ambient
shadows.purpose: The only sanctioned shadow: lifts a modal off its backdrop.

shadows.name: card-legacy
shadows.purpose: Legacy light-mode card shadow. Prefer border-based separation (The Flat Surface Rule).

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: State-change transition duration; keep in the 150-250ms band.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one primary action on a view.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #6366f1; color: #fff; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #4f46e5; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #a5b4fc; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #4338ca; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Subtle Button
components.description: Secondary indigo action; quiet at rest.
components.css: .ds-btn-subtle { background: transparent; color: #4f46e5; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-subtle:hover { background: #eef2ff; color: #4338ca; } .ds-btn-subtle:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #c7d2fe; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Status / limit pill; always carries a word, never color alone.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.375rem; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 0.125rem 0.625rem; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; } .ds-badge--success { background: #dcfce7; color: #15803d; } .ds-badge--warning { background: #fef3c7; color: #b45309; } .ds-badge--danger { background: #fee2e2; color: #b91c1c; }

components.name: Data Panel Card
components.description: Flat surface separated by a border, not a shadow.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #fff; color: #1f2937; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 16px; padding: 20px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1rem; } .ds-card__title { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; margin: 0; } .ds-card__body { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Floating-Label Input
components.description: Label rests inside, floats up on focus/value.
components.css: .ds-field { position: relative; display: block; } .ds-field__input { width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #1f2937; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1rem 0.75rem 0.5rem; transition: border-color 200ms, box-shadow 200ms; } .ds-field__input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #6366f1; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #c7d2fe; } .ds-field__label { position: absolute; left: 0.75rem; top: 0.9rem; color: #6b7280; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; pointer-events: none; transition: transform 200ms, color 200ms, font-size 200ms; transform-origin: left top; } .ds-field__input:focus + .ds-field__label, .ds-field__input:not(:placeholder-shown) + .ds-field__label { transform: translateY(-0.65rem) scale(0.8); color: #4f46e5; }

components.name: Underline Tab (active)
components.description: Active tab: indigo text + indigo underline; tablist scrolls when it overflows.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; flex-wrap: nowrap; overflow-x: auto; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; } .ds-tab { flex: 0 0 auto; background: none; border: 0; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #6b7280; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-tab:hover { color: #1f2937; } .ds-tab--active { color: #6366f1; border-bottom-color: #6366f1; }

components.name: Fuel Build-up Line
components.description: Signature pattern: a mono figure ladder with rule-lined subtotals and running totals.
components.html:
Trip10.4t
Min takeoff12.4t
components.css: .ds-fuel { display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .ds-fuel__row { display: flex; align-items: baseline; justify-content: space-between; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 0.375rem 0; } .ds-fuel__label { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; color: #6b7280; } .ds-fuel__val { font-family: 'Roboto Mono', monospace; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #374151; } .ds-fuel__unit { margin-left: 0.125rem; font-size: 10px; opacity: 0.6; } .ds-fuel__row--subtotal { margin-top: 0.25rem; border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb; padding-top: 0.5rem; } .ds-fuel__row--subtotal .ds-fuel__label { font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; color: #4b5563; } .ds-fuel__row--subtotal .ds-fuel__val { font-weight: 700; color: #1f2937; }

narrative.overview: Flight Tracker is dispatch and cockpit tooling for people who run a virtual airline for the realism of it. The interface reads like the paperwork of a real operation — a flight plan, a loadsheet, a fuel log: exact figures, labeled units, visible subtotals, nothing decorative between the reader and the number. The document is the interface; authority comes from being correct and legible under density, not from chrome. A single indigo carries action and current selection; everything else is a disciplined gray scale that lets tabular data be the loudest thing on screen.
keyCharacteristics: Document-grade: figures are the product; present them exactly, unit-labeled, with derivations shown.
Restrained color: indigo for action and selection only; gray does the structural work.
Flat and honest: borders and tone convey depth, not shadow.
Dense but legible: built for scanning many values at once.
Dual-theme: light and dark ship together and both hold WCAG 2.1 AA.

rules.name: The Rare Accent Rule
rules.body: Instrument Indigo appears on ≤10% of any screen — actions and the one current selection. If two things on a panel are indigo, one is wrong.

rules.name: The Signal-Not-Decoration Rule
rules.body: Color only ever means something. Never encode meaning in hue alone — a limit breach or emergency needs a word or icon beside the color.

rules.name: The Tabular Figures Rule
rules.body: Any number a user compares or sums is set in Roboto Mono with tabular-nums, right-aligned, unit-suffixed. Never set comparable figures in the proportional body font.

rules.name: The Micro-Caps Rule
rules.body: Uppercase tracked labels are the connective tissue; keep tracking ≥ 0.06em and never use them for reading-length text.

rules.name: The Flat Surface Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Separate them with a 1px border and a one-step tonal change, never a drop shadow. The only exception is a modal lifting off its backdrop.

dos: Do set every comparable figure in Roboto Mono with tabular-nums, unit-suffixed and column-aligned.
Do keep Instrument Indigo to ≤10% of a screen — action and current selection only.
Do separate surfaces with a 1px border and a tonal step; keep them flat.
Do show the derivation of computed values (subtotals, running totals, build-ups) so numbers are verifiable.
Do pair every status color with a word or icon, and hold 4.5:1 body-text contrast in both light and dark.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion; keep transitions in the 150-250ms state-change band.
donts: Don't build a flashy gamer / sci-fi HUD — no neon, no glows, no aggressive dark-mode theatrics, no fake-cockpit skeuomorphism.
Don't add drop shadows to separate panels; use borders and tone (modals lifting off the backdrop are the sole exception).
Don't use muted gray (gray-400 or lighter) for body-length text on tinted near-white surfaces — it fails contrast.
Don't set numbers a user compares or sums in the proportional body font.
Don't reach for a modal by reflex — exhaust inline and progressive-disclosure alternatives first.
Don't encode meaning in color alone (a red border with no label is not a message).
Don't let a second element compete with the primary indigo action on a view.
